channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: World Cup teams will be paid a record $871 million: Each team will get $12.5 million as a bare minimum for playing and preparation money
item.description: The winners of the 2026 FIFA World Cup will take home an eye-watering $50 million, while those in last will get an extra $9 million.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Daron Acemoglu on the brainless AI discourse, the myth of capitalism and the Gen Z revolution risk
item.description: The MIT economist and Nobel Prize winner doesn't mince words: most of what you're reading about AI is wrong, and the stakes for getting it right are enormous.

item.title: The national debt's 20-year deadline and baby boomers' spending problem: a lot of incentive for every generation to try to pass a big bill
item.description: Kent Smetters, faculty director of the Penn Wharton Budget Model, estimates that we spend 6x more on older than young people, or actually more like 10x.

item.title: Sam Altman predicts AI will surpass human intelligence by 2030; rival billionaires expect it even sooner
item.description: The OpenAI CEO stated that GPT-5 is already more intelligent than he is.
